1: direct May God be gracious to us and bless us and make his face to shine upon us, The Aaronic blessing (Numbers 6:24-26) is fulfilled in Christ: the blessing of Abraham comes to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus (Galatians 3:14). The face of God shining — God who said let light shine out of darkness, has shone in our hearts to give the light of the knowledge of the glory of God in the face of Jesus Christ (2 Corinthians 4:6). Christ is the shining face of God.
2: direct that your way may be known on earth, your saving power among all nations. Christ declares: I am the way, and the truth, and the life (John 14:6). His saving power is proclaimed among all nations through the Great Commission (Matthew 28:19-20). The way of God known on earth is the way of the cross and resurrection — the saving power that extends to every nation.
3: direct Let the peoples praise you, O God; let all the peoples praise you! Paul quotes this verse in Romans 15:11 as part of his catena demonstrating that the Gentiles were always meant to glorify God alongside Israel: Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles, and let all the peoples extol him. The universal praise is the telos of Christ's mission — the nations brought in to glorify the God of Israel through Jesus Christ.
4: shadow Let the nations be glad and sing for joy, for you judge the peoples with equity and guide the nations upon earth. The equitable judgment and guidance of the nations is vested in the risen Christ: he has fixed a day on which he will judge the world in righteousness by a man whom he has appointed (Acts 17:31). The nations' gladness is eschatological — the joy of receiving just governance from the one King.
5: direct Let the peoples praise you, O God; let all the peoples praise you! The refrain repeated — the insistence of universal praise. Paul's citations in Romans 15:9-11 culminate in this missional vision: Christ has become a servant to the circumcised so that the Gentiles might glorify God (Romans 15:8-9). Every repetition of the refrain is a prayer that the Great Commission reaches its completion.
6: shadow The earth has yielded its increase; God, our God, shall bless us. The earth's yield reaches its eschatological fullness in Christ the firstfruits (1 Corinthians 15:20,23): his resurrection is the first harvest of the new creation. The blessing God gives is the blessing of the Spirit poured out through Christ — the firstfruits of the Spirit (Romans 8:23) guaranteeing the full harvest to come.
7: shadow God shall bless us; let all the ends of the earth fear him! The final verse completes the universalizing arc: the fear of the LORD spreading to all the ends of the earth. Acts 1:8 is the commission that drives this: you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the end of the earth. God's blessing through Christ propels the church to the ends of the earth in holy reverence.
